acid_or_complexing_summary: Citric acid complexing; metal ion:citric acid = 1:1.2; solution pH about 1.0
acid_treatment_purpose: complex metal ions; prevent Mo precipitation; promote homogeneous gel
calcination_condition: citrate precursor thermally decomposed in air at 700 °C for 2 h
activation_summary: ground to fine powder before thermal decomposition
phase_or_state_summary: separate MgMoO4/MgMo2O7/MoO3 phases observed; Fe mainly oxidized
dispersion_summary: non-uniform distribution and agglomerated catalyst particles
expected_CNT_type_bias: mixed thick CNTs, carbon fibers and some t-MWCNTs
catalyst_assessment: [review_assessment] poor control catalyst; low surface area and phase separation correlate with low yield
evidence_text: Air decomposition produced low BET area, large pores and non-uniform Fe-Mo dispersion; Table 1 reports 60 m²/g.
evidence_location: PDF pp. 2-5; Section 2.1; Section 3.2; Table 1; Figs. 1-3
notes: Bracketed MgO-only support values from Table 1 were not entered as catalyst values.

acid_or_complexing_summary: Citric acid complexing; metal ion:citric acid = 1:1.2; solution pH about 1.0
acid_treatment_purpose: complex metal ions; prevent Mo precipitation; promote homogeneous gel
calcination_condition: citrate precursor thermally decomposed in Ar at 500 sccm and 700 °C for 2 h
activation_summary: ground to fine powder before thermal decomposition
phase_or_state_summary: no separate Fe2O3 or MoOx phase detected; Fe-Mo nanoclusters dispersed in MgO
dispersion_summary: well-dispersed 3-6 nm Fe-Mo particles; Mo-rich clusters
catalyst_assessment: [review_assessment] best route in this paper; high BET area and fine pores correlate with highest yield and uniform product
evidence_text: Ar decomposition produced 3-6 nm dispersed particles and 202 m²/g BET area; Table 1 and TEM support the assessment.
evidence_location: PDF pp. 2-5; Section 2.1; Section 3.2; Table 1; Figs. 1-3
notes: TEM-EDS spot ratio was Mo-rich, but it was not treated as the bulk catalyst composition.

acid_or_complexing_summary: Citric acid complexing; metal ion:citric acid = 1:1.2; solution pH about 1.0
acid_treatment_purpose: complex metal ions; prevent Mo precipitation; promote homogeneous gel
calcination_condition: citrate precursor thermally decomposed in H2 at 500 sccm and 700 °C for 2 h
activation_summary: ground to fine powder before thermal decomposition
phase_or_state_summary: Fe-Mo nanoclusters dispersed in MgO; Mo2C phase observed under reducing decomposition
dispersion_summary: well-dispersed 3-6 nm Fe-Mo particles; Mo-rich clusters
catalyst_assessment: [review_assessment] strong comparison route; similar dispersion to SG-2 but lower product yield and slightly larger tubes
evidence_text: H2 decomposition produced 3-6 nm dispersed particles and 210 m²/g BET area; Mo2C was also observed.
evidence_location: PDF pp. 2-5; Section 2.1; Section 3.2; Table 1; Figs. 1-3
notes: TEM-EDS spot ratio was Mo-rich, but it was not treated as the bulk catalyst composition.
